title: -Contract No. 32200207 Amendment: Telesis for Plan Checking Services

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to execute an amendment to Contract
No. 32200207 with Telesis to add additional funds, increasing the contract amount by
$100,000 to a new total not-to-exceed contract amount of $1,600,000.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
The full costs for outside plan checking services are covered by plan check fees paid by
applicants. The consultants are used on an as-needed basis, which varies based on the
availability of in-house resources, as well as on the level and complexity of construction
projects. The consultants invoice the City by project, based on work completed. All
contract expenditures are fully covered from fees paid by project applicants.
Funding for the proposed amendment to this contract is available in the FY 2025 budget
from the Permit Service Center Fund in account number 621-53-585-634-0000-000-
472-612990-. No other funding is required.
sections.current_situation:
The Building and Safety Division has issued a Request for Proposals (RFP) for a new
set of plan check consultants. Staff expect this RFP process to be completed, and new
contracts recommended to Council for approval, by June 2025.
Until new contracts are in place, staff propose increasing the amount of the existing
contract with Telesis. This will enable Telesis to continue providing plan check services
for the City upon request and at project applicant expense. The services provided allow
the Building and Safety Division to maintain a turnaround time for plan review that
meets the City’s performance objectives and the needs of the community. Consultants
can be used flexibly when volume is higher than staff can handle, and to maintain
necessary services in the face of unforeseeable staff shortages. Consultants also
provide special expertise when needed, such as reviews for large and complex projects
such as high-rise buildings, the soft-story program, advanced or alternate building
systems such as heavy timber projects and other customized construction.
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7000 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510) 981-7099
E-Mail: manager@berkeleyca.gov Website: https://berkeleyca.gov/

Amend Contract with Telesis for Plan Check Services CONSENT CALENDAR
April 29, 2025
sections.background:
On May 16, 2022, following an RFP procurement process and contract authorization by
Council, the City Manager entered into a contract with Telesis to provide plan check
services for a contract period of three years in amount not to exceed $1,500,000
(Resolution No. 70,230-N.S.).
On March 12, 2025, the Planning Department issued a Request for Proposals (Spec
No. 25-11713-C) for plan checking services.
On March 17, 2025, the City extended the existing contract with Telesis for a period of
six months, for a new termination date of September 30, 2025.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
There are no identifiable environmental effects or opportunities associated with the
subject of this report.
sections.rationale:
Without this expert help, plan review time would be increased substantially, particularly
on large and complex projects, and special expertise would have to be contracted on a
case-by-case basis, causing extensive delays.
The City is satisfied with the services provided by Telesis to date under this contract,
and the proposed amendment will provide the consultant with additional capacity to
provide the services needed while the Planning Department reviews proposals in
response to the issued RFP and completes the contract process.
sections.alternatives:
None.
CONTACT PERSON
Jordan Klein, Director, Planning and Development Department, 510-981-7534
David Lopez, Manager, Building & Safety Division, 510-981-7441
sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 2
Page 3 of 3
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT NO. 32200207 AMENDMENT: TELESIS FOR PLAN CHECK SERVICES
WHEREAS, the Planning Department issued a Request for Proposals on October 5, 2021
(Spec No. 22-11467-C) for plan checking services; and
WHEREAS, based on written proposals received, staff selected Telesis to provide such
services; and
WHEREAS, on May 16, 2022 the City Council adopted Resolution 70,230-N.S.,
authorizing the City manager to enter a contract in an amount not-to-exceed $1,500,000
with Telesis to provide such services; and
WHEREAS, the City extended the contract for a period of six months, for a new
termination date of September 30, 2025; and
WHEREAS, expected City needs for external plan check services will require work from
Telesis which exceeds its current contract not-to-exceed amount; and
WHEREAS, additional funding in the amount of $100,000 is currently available in the
Permit Service Center Fund (621) for this work.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager is authorized to execute an amendment to Contract No. 32200207 with
Telesis to provide plan check services for an additional amount of $100,000, to a new
total contract not-to-exceed amount of $1,600,000, for the period extending through
September 30, 2025.
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED, that a record signature copy of said contract and any
amendments will be on file in the Office of the City Clerk.
